text: it takes two to tango and both parties share the responsibility

text: the ball is in your court so make the next move carefully

text: bite the bullet and get the most difficult task out of the way first

text: every cloud has a silver lining so stay optimistic

text: do not put all your eggs in one basket

text: break a leg means good luck especially before a performance

text: the early bird catches the worm means being early gives you an advantage

text: rome was not built in a day means great things take time to achieve

text: miss the boat means to miss an opportunity

text: no pain no gain means effort is required to achieve something worthwhile

text: pull someone's leg means to joke with someone

text: speak of the devil means the person you were talking about has just appeared

text: steal someone's thunder means to take credit for someone else's achievement

text: the tip of the iceberg means a small visible part of a much larger issue

text: wrap your head around means to understand something complicated

text: you reap what you sow means the consequences of your actions will come back to you

text: go the extra mile means to make more effort than is expected

text: keep your chin up means to stay positive during hard times

text: leave no stone unturned means to search everywhere and try everything

text: the whole nine yards means everything or all of it

text: under your nose means right in front of you but not noticed

text: variety is the spice of life means new experiences make life interesting

text: walking on eggshells means being very careful about what you say or do

text: bite off more than you can chew means to take on more than you can handle

text: call it a day means to stop working on something

text: down to earth means to be practical and realistic

text: face the music means to accept the unpleasant consequences of your actions

text: jump on the bandwagon means to join a popular trend or activity

text: keep an eye on means to watch carefully

text: let bygones be bygones means to forget about past disagreements

text: practice what you preach means to act according to the advice you give others

text: raise the bar means to set higher standards

text: so far so good means everything has gone well until now

text: water under the bridge means something in the past that is no longer important

text: you can lead a horse to water but you cannot make it drink

text: a penny for your thoughts means asking someone what they are thinking

text: blow off steam means to release tension or pent-up energy

text: curiosity killed the cat means being too inquisitive can lead to trouble

text: do not count your chickens before they hatch means do not assume future success

text: easy come easy go means something gained without effort is quickly lost

text: fit as a fiddle means to be in very good health

text: get a taste of your own medicine means to experience the same treatment you gave others

text: hit the ground running means to start something with great energy and enthusiasm

text: look before you leap means to think carefully before acting

text: the devil is in the details means small things can cause big problems

text: time flies when you are having fun means enjoyable activities make time pass quickly

text: two wrongs don't make a right means revenge is not justified

text: when in Rome do as the Romans do means to follow local customs

text: you cannot have your cake and eat it too means you cannot have it both ways

text: a watched pot never boils means time seems to go slower when you wait
